# WI-QUIC-RFC9002-0003 - QUIC RFC 9002 Retry Recovery Reset Work Item

## Summary

Close the RFC 9002 Section 6.3 Retry slice by resetting recovery and congestion state while preserving the handshake transcript that feeds the client bootstrap replay.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P3-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P3-0002
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9002-S6P3-0003

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-RFC9002-0003

## Planned Changes

- Reset the client sender and recovery runtime when Retry is processed.
- Keep the TLS handshake transcript in place so the replay bootstrap can resend the original ClientHello.
- Tighten the requirement-home tests so they assert concrete runtime state before and after Retry.

## Out of Scope

- RFC 9002 Retry RTT-estimation follow-ons.
- Broader Retry policy, multi-Retry handling, or public API widening.
- RFC 9000 transport framing and RFC 9001 packet-protection behavior that already belong to their own slices.

## Verification Plan

Run the Retry-focused requirement-home tests that cover rejection as ACK, recovery reset, and handshake transcript retention.
Run the targeted dotnet test slice for the RFC 9002 Retry family and the Retry integrity unit tests.
Render the trace markdown siblings from the canonical JSON artifacts so the downstream reports stay in sync.

## Completion Notes

Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
